note: Advisory sandbox build. The deliverable is a report-only forbidden-marker PR grep gate (script + non-blocking pull_request workflow proposal). Its proof is the scanner's own dogfood: a clean sample passes (exit 0), a minimal mutation embedding the forbidden marker is flagged (exit 1), and the workflow YAML is asserted to carry every CI guard. Never a bare governance PASS.
title: forbidden-marker PR grep gate: report-only scanner + non-blocking pull_request workflow proposal

ground_truth.note: marker is named in copilot-instructions.md as forbidden in active config / executable code paths; the scanner assembles it from fragments so the scanner source does not itself embed the literal string.

subject.note: reads the ground-truth marker only; all writes confined to catalog/builds/CI-5.

what_it_proves: the report-only scanner scans a given file list for the forbidden active-config marker and prints a JSON report (default) or Markdown summary
POSITIVE - a clean sample file is NOT flagged: clean=true, hit_count=0, exit 0
NEGATIVE - a sample file carrying the forbidden marker IS flagged: clean=false, correct file+line reported, exit nonzero (1)

red-before-green minimality - the SAME clean file plus exactly one injected marker line flips clean->flagged; reversing the single line reproduces the file byte-for-byte and the clean verdict returns (same scanner, opposite verdicts, no strawman)
the scanner is report-only: reads files only, performs no writes / no network / no git mutation / no receipt / no supabase / no block-merge
GUARD contents:read - the workflow declares permissions: contents: read only, with no write scope
GUARD pull_request-only - on: pull_request only; pull_request_target / push / schedule are absent
GUARD no-secrets/env - no secrets.* references and no env: blocks / credential tokens anywhere in the YAML
GUARD non-blocking - the scan step is continue-on-error: true, findings post to the job summary, and the workflow performs no merge / enforces no required status
GUARD report-only-invocation - the script is invoked with --json / --md only, never --write-supabase / --write-receipt / --fail-on-block-merge
chess_patches_applied: positive fixture is a real clean config sample; negative fixture is a single-line mutation embedding the real forbidden marker - the scanner is never relaxed to pass
marker assembled from fragments in scanner + test sources so neither self-flags (the literal appears only in the intentionally-dirty data fixture)
YAML guard checks strip full-line comments first, so words appearing only in explanatory comments (e.g. 'pull_request_target') cannot spoof the guard assertions
verdict vocab CHECK_OK / CHECK_REJECTED, origin=sandbox-advisory / authority=none; never a bare governance PASS
locks_respected: append-only (copilot-instructions.md read-only; all writes confined to catalog/builds/CI-5)
no-live (no network / no secrets / HOME redirected to temp for the self-runner; scanner does file reads only)
CI isolation - YAML authored as a PROPOSAL in the build dir, NOT written into .github/; no existing workflow touched (brain-loop-autorun and others untouched)
worktree not pushed; no git write commands; other lanes' files and untracked drops (graph-out/, receipts/l0-*, scripts/*repo_graph*) neither read nor staged

results.red_run_canary: FIRED (asserting a marker-bearing fixture is CLEAN raised AssertionError, exit 1)
results.pollution_outside_build_dir: none (git status --porcelain tracked changes outside CI-5 == 0)

exit_gate_B4: CI-5 done - report-only forbidden-marker scanner flags a marker-bearing file and clears a clean one by minimal mutation, and its non-blocking pull_request workflow proposal is asserted to carry contents:read-only, pull_request-only, no-secrets/env, and non-blocking guards.
next_action: on founder approval, reconcile the YAML proposal to .github/workflows/ and the scanner to scripts/; optionally widen the scanned set to the full tree on a scheduled advisory run (still report-only).
